lesson.title: TCP/IP Protocol Suite
lesson.objective: Describe the four layers of the TCP/IP model
Explain how TCP ensures reliable, ordered delivery
Distinguish between TCP and UDP and know when to use each
lesson.note: TCP/IP (Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol) is the foundational protocol suite of the internet. Unlike the 7-layer OSI model, TCP/IP uses a simpler 4-layer model.
lesson.diagram: OSI Model TCP/IP Model Protocols ───────────── ──────────── ────────── Application ┐ Presentation ├──► Application ──► HTTP, FTP, DNS, SMTP Session ┘ Transport ───► Transport ──► TCP, UDP Network ───► Internet ──► IP, ICMP, ARP Data Link ┐ Physical └──► Network ──► Ethernet, Wi-Fi
lesson.definition: A connection-oriented protocol that guarantees reliable, ordered delivery of data. It uses a three-way handshake to establish connections and acknowledges received packets.
lesson.example: Before data transfer, TCP establishes a connection: 1. SYN: Client sends "I want to connect" (SYN flag) 2. SYN-ACK: Server replies "OK, I'm ready" (SYN + ACK flags) 3. ACK: Client confirms "Great, let's go" (ACK flag) After this handshake, reliable data transfer begins.
lesson.codeblock: TCP Handshake: Client Server |----SYN------------>| (Seq=100) |<---SYN-ACK---------| (Seq=300, Ack=101) |----ACK------------>| (Ack=301) |====DATA TRANSFER===|
lesson.definition: A connectionless protocol that sends data without establishing a connection or guaranteeing delivery. It is faster than TCP but less reliable.
lesson.note: Use TCP when data integrity is critical (web browsing, email, file transfer). Use UDP when speed matters more than reliability (video streaming, online gaming, DNS queries, VoIP).
lesson.example: Every device on the internet has an IP address. IPv4 addresses are 32-bit numbers written as four octets: 192.168.1.1. IPv6 uses 128-bit addresses: 2001:0db8:85a3:0000:0000:8a2e:0370:7334.

question.text: Which protocol would you use for a video streaming application where some packet loss is acceptable?
How many steps are in the TCP three-way handshake?
UDP guarantees that all packets arrive in order.

lesson.exercise: Using Wireshark (or a network capture tool), capture traffic and identify: (a) At least one TCP connection showing the three-way handshake (b) At least one UDP communication (e.g., DNS) Compare the packet headers of TCP vs UDP segments.

summary.item: TCP/IP has 4 layers: Application, Transport, Internet, Network Access
TCP is reliable and connection-oriented; UDP is fast but connectionless
TCP uses a three-way handshake (SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK) to establish connections
IP provides addressing (IPv4: 32-bit, IPv6: 128-bit) and routing
Choose TCP for reliability, UDP for low-latency real-time applications
